column3: Install setup and get safety review complete.

column4: Safety Patrol Completed by Dragoslav Lazic
first effort is to verify if Beam Wirechamber are functional
One chamber (E) most downstream is working.  Pulses are too large by a factor of two, so attenuation needed
Two put the chamber into the DRS1 will require x2 attenuation of the signals.   Onur is going to get a NIM unit to take care of this.
x6 dB attenuators are needed for each channel. So five more are needed

Attempt to see any meaningul signals from the beam chambers lead to nothing.  Occssional occilation signals.  Given this lack of progress, we have. elected to return to the RADiCAL alignment and to bring it into opperation.
Berkan and James discussed vertical offset of RADiCAL center - James earlier reported an incorrect height of 78 mm.  The correct height is 83 mm from the motion table. - JW
One chamber functioinal (E) downstream nearest RADiCAL module.
column7: But signals are too large for DRS.   So 6dB attenuation needed.  We had two, sand the y channels 1 and 3, where 3 is an anode signal are implemented in DRS1.  We will need to find 5 more of these during the day shift.

column4: Carlos setting up codes for data taking

column3: Alexi added atenuator to WC signals and plug them into DRS
Thomas added fan to radical box for downstream card
All looks good and well timed.  We need to fix:
Plug 1x1 signal into both DRS units. So that we can start the synchonization
There is a strange noise in trigger. See picture
Something funny with 1 RADICAL HIGAIN Channel
Berkan, Onur, Selbi and Thomas plugged 1x1 and 2x2 signals to DRS. Fixed noise problem in TR0

column6: We notice that DRS0 got into crazy mode forr very few events
Took Another run. All events were fine now
We notice that now DRS0 and DRS1 got into crazy mode forr very few events
column12: Note to self: I think this crazy events is the DRS attempting to read while in busy. Once handshake is stablish I will confirm

column3: Message from Sasha: Habemus WireChambers! Signals look healthy. We start to analyze. Problem Wirechamber mapping is a bit unexpected. MCP signal is to small and narrow.
There is a 150 ns window between trigger and veto. Probably better to fill with narrow self veto.
column7: This was added in at 5:30 AM - BK, OP, RR

column3: After several power cycles from DRS and NIM crate, DRS0 does not come back NOR noise in trigger goes away. Decided to reboot the machine
Access to plug DRS0 and DRS1 with USB cable. Only DRS0 could be plug. However iDRS0 still not triggering.
Channel Mapping posted in documents /images.   This  reviesion including exactly where the beam chamber channels were inserted.
Shift Schedule posted dcuments/images.  Please alert RR of any corrections.
Berkan and Onur called to check trigger crate due to alarm.  Power/heating issues suspected.
Reparied.   Veto added to block incidental triggers between event trigger and arrival of back pressure pulses from CAEN DRS0 and DRS1.
CCC called to understand why we have not been getting beam., since about 0300 except we have been having equipment issues that distracted our attention.
Berkan with CCC found that some radiation monitor in the beam line had indicated somethine and would not reset.   Now reset with beam delivery.
Revised Run Plan posted to site.
Berkan started a Data run for test.  looks like the two DRS units are seeing the same number of events and units are assumed to be sychronized.
Beam currently down for at least 30 mins
Beam back, finished an access to check D0-G0-C1, fixed it but may fail again. Returning to R100 data taking
Operating normally again, had to cancel run 100 and restart with run 101

DRS Hung again at around ~100 k events, stopped run, reduced events / run to 50k for the near-term, D0-G0-C1 back to broken, think it's a loose / disconnected cap or similar, wil attempt a repair later today
Reviewing data from Run 102, then will rename and then restart voltage scan

Finished access to slightly move the MCP to make it more aligned with the RADiCAL Module, raised MCP Bias to -3kV, and confirmed output cap on D0-G0-C1 appears the problem, will go back inside after Alexi arrives to solder it again.
Beam still down, finished repair of G0-G0-C1 channel, think it's good at this point. Problem was a capacitor. We'll know better once beam returns and we can take a few spills
Beam has returned, channel D0-G0-C1 is fixed, taking some test runs t100, t102, will return to main sequence after a discussion with Sasha
Taking runs at 30k / run due to corruption issue. I found that if the DRS locks and you stop getting triggers you can avoid power-cycling by reloading the A2818 Driver
Finished ~1 million at 125 GeV for 41.25V, ran some bias tests and in discussion with Randy going to run higher at 42.25 V and take 1 million more events before moving to a lower energy
Beam went down for about 45 minutes, now back running
Started data taking of electrons at 125 GeV. All deterctors work fine

column7: 1) From time to time the crate in the ebamline will overheat. At that time the red light in the bottom of the crate will turn on and you will hear a buzzing

column8: How to know that is happening: You will see a noise in the triggers: DRS will trigger even without beam at around 100 Hz
How to solve: Make access and recycle power in the crate. Then ask for beam back

column7: 2) From time to time the CONET will stop commuinication with the DRS units.

column8: How to know that is happening: You will not been able to start the DAQ
How to solve: Resintall the CAEN driver

column3: Doing runs of 30k each to ease handling

column5: run one of the commented python commands
